generatedSentence: In a timeless, colossal multiplex, an emergent elixir of nonpareil quality was secretly bottled in the frontside garages, attracting ardent, often alcoholic, patrons. The police roller, a vanilla-flavored knobstick leader nicknamed "Owl," would often vacillate, choosing to pick up bloody teasers instead of investigating the source, despite the crystal alarm bells ringing vicariously. An earthworm cleaner found an antique bottle, a chilling appetizer of the coming multiplier devilry. This element of supply, a virus-like substance, was mistakenly turning the population into gladiators, their need for it becoming a hazard. Jobless harvesters, once productive, now performed freaky krump antics, playing out a dangerous volley for their dealers. The usually nurturing medical staff, now gray and excited, prescribed epidural nourishment for lungs strained from synchronized howitzer blasts, a dangerous summation of their new binary existence, below the latitude of control. The situation nationwide became truly remarkable; integrated castles rose from the valleys, their geographic coordinates synched to ancient visions of a sanctified northern aurora. PI Dick, nicknamed Hotspur, a resourceful leader with a legendary flute, found himself wanting to outfox this colossal threat. He learned that rancid anteater bark was being dredged from beneath the medieval kitchens, creating a mammoth, gritty component for the elixir, fueling the population's hunger. In a decisive, abrupt move, Dick, with his morningstars and judicial insight, creatively planned to mythify the source, targeting the igloos where the developers lived like gardeners among reptiles. He knew the rodeo-like chaos in the cafeteria and garages had to end. With a final, triumphant nod, he moved to cut off the supply, ensuring the nonpareil elixir would no longer dictate the future, restoring order to a world on the brink.
panelSentences: In a timeless, colossal multiplex, an emergent elixir of nonpareil quality was secretly bottled in the frontside garages, attracting ardent, often alcoholic, patrons. The police roller, a vanilla-flavored knobstick leader nicknamed "Owl," would often vacillate, choosing to pick up bloody teasers instead of investigating the source, despite the crystal alarm bells ringing vicariously. Jobless harvesters, once productive, now performed freaky krump antics, playing out a dangerous volley for their dealers. The usually nurturing medical staff, now gray and excited, prescribed epidural nourishment for lungs strained from synchronized howitzer blasts, a dangerous summation of their new binary existence, below the latitude of control.

The situation nationwide became truly remarkable; integrated castles rose from the valleys, their geographic coordinates synched to ancient visions of a sanctified northern aurora. PI Dick, nicknamed Hotspur, a resourceful leader with a legendary flute, found himself wanting to outfox this colossal threat. He learned that rancid anteater bark was being dredged from beneath the medieval kitchens, creating a mammoth, gritty component for the elixir, fueling the population's hunger.

In a decisive, abrupt move, Dick, with his morningstars and judicial insight, creatively planned to mythify the source, targeting the igloos where the developers lived like gardeners among reptiles. He knew the rodeo-like chaos in the cafeteria and garages had to end. rawPanelSentences: In a timeless, colossal multiplex, an emergent elixir of nonpareil quality was secretly bottled in the frontside garages, attracting ardent, often alcoholic, patrons. The police roller, a vanilla-flavored knobstick leader nicknamed "Owl," would often vacillate, choosing to pick up bloody teasers instead of investigating the source, despite the crystal alarm bells ringing vicariously. An earthworm cleaner found an antique bottle, a chilling appetizer of the coming multiplier devilry.

This element of supply, a virus-like substance, was mistakenly turning the population into gladiators, their need for it becoming a hazard. Jobless harvesters, once productive, now performed freaky krump antics, playing out a dangerous volley for their dealers. The usually nurturing medical staff, now gray and excited, prescribed epidural nourishment for lungs strained from synchronized howitzer blasts, a dangerous summation of their new binary existence, below the latitude of control.

The situation nationwide became truly remarkable; integrated castles rose from the valleys, their geographic coordinates synched to ancient visions of a sanctified northern aurora. PI Dick, nicknamed Hotspur, a resourceful leader with a legendary flute, found himself wanting to outfox this colossal threat. He learned that rancid anteater bark was being dredged from beneath the medieval kitchens, creating a mammoth, gritty component for the elixir, fueling the population's hunger.

In a decisive, abrupt move, Dick, with his morningstars and judicial insight, creatively planned to mythify the source, targeting the igloos where the developers lived like gardeners among reptiles. He knew the rodeo-like chaos in the cafeteria and garages had to end. With a final, triumphant nod, he moved to cut off the supply, ensuring the nonpareil elixir would no longer dictate the future, restoring order to a world on the brink.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A resourceful, grizzled detective with a silver flute tucked into his belt, wearing a trench coat and a fedora. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Detective 'Dick Hotspur' observes a colossal neon multiplex from a high vantage point, the frontside garages glowing with an eerie purple light, a vintage police roller car parked below. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A group of frenzied figures, appearing as modern gladiators, perform a 'freaky krump' dance in a gritty urban valley, their lungs visibly strained, a binary-coded supply drone hovering above. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Detective Hotspur studies an ancient, glowing map displaying an aurora over futuristic castles, pointing to a 'rancid anteater bark' being dredged from a medieval kitchen below. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Hotspur, wielding glowing morningstars, leads a charge towards futuristic igloos nestled in a landscape dotted with reptilian silhouettes, a look of grim determination on his face.

definitions.definition: To sway unsteadily from one side to the other; oscillate.

definitions.definition: To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.

definitions.definition: (computer engineering) Of an operation, function, procedure or logic gate, taking exactly two operands, arguments, parameters or inputs; having domain of dimension 2.
definitions.example: Division of reals is a binary operation.

definitions.definition: Of data, consisting coded values (e.g. machine code) not interpretable as plain or ASCII text (e.g. source code).
definitions.example: He downloaded the binary distribution for Linux, then burned it to DVD.

definitions.definition: Focusing on two mutually exclusive conditions.
definitions.example: He has a very binary understanding of gender.

definitions.definition: An achromatic colour intermediate between black and white.
An animal or thing of grey colour, such as a horse, badger, or salmon.
An extraterrestrial humanoid with grayish skin, bulbous black eyes, and an enlarged head.
A penny with a tail on both sides, used for cheating.
To become gray.
definitions.example: My hair is beginning to gray.

definitions.definition: To cause to become gray.
To turn progressively older, alluding to graying of hair through aging (used in context of the population of a geographic region)
definitions.example: the graying of America

definitions.definition: To give a soft effect to (a photograph) by covering the negative while printing with a ground-glass plate.
Having a color somewhere between white and black, as the ash of an ember.
Dreary, gloomy.
Having an indistinct, disputed or uncertain quality.
Relating to older people.
definitions.example: the gray dollar, i.e. the purchasing power of the elderly

definitions.definition: To stir the emotions of.
definitions.example: The fireworks which opened the festivities excited anyone present.

definitions.definition: To arouse or bring out (e.g. feelings); to stimulate.
definitions.example: Favoritism tends to excite jealousy in the ones not being favored.

definitions.definition: To cause an electron to move to a higher than normal state; to promote an electron to an outer level.
definitions.example: By applying electric potential to the neon atoms, the electrons become excited, then emit a photon when returning to normal.

definitions.definition: To energize (an electromagnet); to produce a magnetic field in.
definitions.example: to excite a dynamo

definitions.definition: Having great enthusiasm.
definitions.example: He was very excited about his promotion.

definitions.definition: Being in a state of higher energy.
definitions.example: The excited electrons give off light when they drop to a lower energy state.

definitions.definition: Having an erection; erect.
Sexually aroused.
An injection of anaesthetic into the epidural space of the spine, especially associated with pain relief during childbirth.
Situated on or outside the dura mater.
Capable or clever; able to put available resources to efficient or ingenious use; using materials at hand wisely or efficiently.
definitions.example: With a resourceful use of space and a fresh coat of paint, the room became a pleasant library with a comfortable seating area.

definitions.definition: The people living within a political or geographical boundary.
definitions.example: The population of New Jersey will not stand for this!

definitions.definition: (by extension) The people with a given characteristic.
definitions.example: India has the third-largest population of English-speakers in the world.

definitions.definition: A count of the number of residents within a political or geographical boundary such as a town, a nation or the world.
definitions.example: The town’s population is only 243.

definitions.definition: A collection of organisms of a particular species, sharing a particular characteristic of interest, most often that of living in a given area.
definitions.example: A seasonal migration annually changes the populations in two or more biotopes drastically, many twice in opposite senses.

definitions.definition: A group of units (persons, objects, or other items) enumerated in a census or from which a sample is drawn.
The act of filling initially empty items in a collection.
definitions.example: John clicked the Search button and waited for the population of the list to complete.

definitions.definition: A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An instance of castling.
A rook; a chess piece shaped like a castle tower.
A defense structure in shogi formed by defensive pieces surrounding the king.
A close helmet.
Any strong, imposing, and stately mansion.
A small tower, as on a ship, or an elephant's back.
The wicket.
To house or keep in a castle.
To protect or separate in a similar way.
To make into a castle: to build in the form of a castle or add (real or imitation) battlements to an existing building.

(usually intransitive) To move the king 2 squares right or left and, in the same turn, the nearest rook to the far side of the king. The move now has special rules: the king cannot be in, go through, or end in check; the squares between the king and rook must be vacant; and neither piece may have been moved before castling.
(usually intransitive) To create a similar defensive position in Japanese chess through several moves.
To bowl a batsman with a full-length ball or yorker such that the stumps are knocked over.
A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.
A caricature.
(often in plural) A ludicrous gesture or act; ridiculous behaviour; caper.
A grotesque performer or clown, buffoon.
To perform antics, caper.
To make a fool of, to cause to look ridiculous.
To perform (an action) as an antic; to mimic ridiculously.
A pose, often exaggerated, in anticipation of an action; for example, a brief squat before jumping
Someone living in the Middle Ages.
A medieval example (of something aforementioned or understood from context).
definitions.example: Thank God for modern remedies: the medievals were often useless or even harmful.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the Middle Ages, the period from approximately 500 to 1500 AD.
Having characteristics associated with the Middle Ages in popular, modern cultural perception:
A medical examination.
definitions.example: You'll have to get a medical before you apply for that job.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to the practice of medicine.
definitions.example: Do you have any medical experience?

definitions.definition: Intended to have a therapeutic effect; medicinal.
definitions.example: medical marijuana; medical cannabis; medical treatment

definitions.definition: Requiring medical treatment.
definitions.example: A costly medical condition can bankrupt you if it doesn't kill you first.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the state of one's health.
Pertaining to or requiring treatment by other than surgical means.
definitions.example: medical ward

definitions.definition: That provides nourishment; nutritious
In a lower place.
On a lower storey.
Further down.
On a lower deck.
definitions.example: the landlubbers lying down below

definitions.definition: (of a temperature) Below zero.
Lower in spatial position than.
definitions.example: The treasure is buried two meters below the surface.

definitions.definition: Within the writing of a given document which follows a particular appearance of the word "below".
definitions.example: "By their execution hereof, the Parties incur a legal obligation to pass consideration under this Loan Contract as is set forth below."

definitions.definition: Lower in value, price, rank or concentration than.
definitions.example: The temperature is below zero.

definitions.definition: Downstream of.
South of.
Unsuitable to the rank or dignity of; beneath.
definitions.example: Such petty behavior is below me.

definitions.definition: (stage directions) Downstage of.
The angular distance north or south from a planet's equator, measured along the meridian of that particular point.
An imaginary line (in fact a circumference) around a planet running parallel to the planet's equator.
The relative freedom from restrictions; scope to do something.
definitions.example: His parents gave him a great deal of latitude.

definitions.definition: The angular distance of a heavenly body from the ecliptic.
The extent to which a light-sensitive material can be over- or underexposed and still achieve an acceptable result.
Extent or scope; e.g. breadth, width or amplitude.
Krumping: an energetic style of hip-hop dance
Any bird of the family Gruidae, large birds with long legs and a long neck which is extended during flight.
Ardea herodias, the great blue heron.
A mechanical lifting machine or device, often used for lifting heavy loads for industrial or construction purposes.
An iron arm with horizontal motion, attached to the side or back of a fireplace for supporting kettles etc. over the fire.
A siphon, or bent pipe, for drawing liquors out of a cask.
A forked post or projecting bracket to support spars, etc.; generally used in pairs.
To extend (one's neck).
To raise or lower with, or as if with, a crane.
To pull up before a jump.
The cranium.
A male person.
The penis.
A highly contemptible person; a jerk.
definitions.example: That person is such a dick.

definitions.definition: Absolutely nothing.
definitions.example: Last weekend I did dick.

definitions.definition: Sexual intercourse with a man.
To mistreat or take advantage of somebody (often with around or up).
definitions.example: Dude, don't let them dick you around like that!

definitions.definition: (of a man) To have sexual intercourse with.
Resembling a freak.
Odd; bizarre; unusual.
definitions.example: I've heard the props and costumes in this play are quite freaky.

definitions.definition: Scary; frightening.
definitions.example: Have you met the freaky new guy who moved in next door?

definitions.definition: Sexually deviant.
definitions.example: The things she asked me to do were too freaky for me.

definitions.definition: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.
To include as a constituent part or functionality.
definitions.example: They were keen to integrate their new skills into the performance.

definitions.definition: To indicate the whole of; to give the sum or total of; as, an integrating anemometer, one that indicates or registers the entire action of the wind in a given time.
To subject to the operation of integration; to find the integral of.
To desegregate, as a school or neighborhood.
definitions.example: The refugees were well integrated into the community.

definitions.definition: To combine compatible elements in order to incorporate them.
Composed and coordinated to form a whole
Characterized by racial integration
A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
(plural) Capacity for exercise or exertion; breath.
definitions.example: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.

definitions.definition: That which supplies oxygen or fresh air, such as trees, parklands, forest, etc., to a place.
To act in a manner such that one has fun; to engage in activities expressly for the purpose of recreation or entertainment.
definitions.example: They played long and hard.

definitions.definition: To perform in (a sport); to participate in (a game).
definitions.example: He plays on three teams

definitions.definition: To take part in amorous activity; to make love.
To act as the indicated role, especially in a performance.
definitions.example: He plays the King, and she's the Queen.

definitions.definition: (heading) To produce music or theatre.
(heading) To behave in a particular way.
To move in any manner; especially, to move regularly with alternate or reciprocating motion; to operate.
definitions.example: He played the torch beam around the room.

definitions.definition: To move to and fro.
To put in action or motion.
definitions.example: to play a trump in a card game

definitions.definition: To keep in play, as a hooked fish in order to land it.
To manipulate, deceive, or swindle someone.
definitions.example: You played me!

definitions.definition: (gerund of play) An occasion on which something, such as a song or show, is played.
To beat in a competition of wits
A restaurant in which customers select their food at a counter then carry it on a tray to a table to eat
A dining area in an institution where meals may be purchased (as above), provided, or brought in from elsewhere
A sending or mission (to do or accomplish something).
An official charge or authority to do something, often used of military officers.
definitions.example: David received his commission after graduating from West Point.

definitions.definition: The thing to be done as agent for another.
definitions.example: I have three commissions for the city.

definitions.definition: A body or group of people, officially tasked with carrying out a particular function.
definitions.example: The company's sexual harassment commission made sure that every employee completed the on-line course.

definitions.definition: A fee charged by an agent or broker for carrying out a transaction.
definitions.example: The real-estate broker charged a four percent commission for their knowledge on bidding for commercial properties; for their intellectual perspective on making a formal offer and the strategy to obtain a mutually satisfying deal with the seller in favour of the buyer.

definitions.definition: The act of committing (e.g. a crime).
definitions.example: the commission, preparation or instigation of an act of terrorism

definitions.definition: To send or officially charge someone or some group to do something.
definitions.example: James Bond was commissioned with recovering the secret documents.

definitions.definition: To place an order for (often piece of art)
definitions.example: He commissioned a replica of the Mona Lisa for his living room, but the painter gave up after six months.

definitions.definition: To put into active service
definitions.example: The aircraft carrier was commissioned in 1944, during WWII.

definitions.definition: An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.

Any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus, of large, usually hairy, elephant-like mammals with long curved tusks and an inclined back, which became extinct with the last retreat of ice age glaciers during the late Pleistocene period, and are known from fossils, frozen carcasses, and Paleolithic cave paintings found in North America and Eurasia.
A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.
An inhabitant of the northern regions.
The northern pike.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the north;northerly.
(of a wind) Blowing from the north; northerly.
Characteristic of the North of England (usually capitalised)
definitions.example: Les Dawson was a famous northern comedian.

definitions.definition: The short, loud, explosive sound uttered by a dog, a fox, and some other animals.
An abrupt loud vocal utterance.
To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).
definitions.example: The neighbour's dog is always barking.

definitions.definition: To make a clamor; to make importunate outcries.
To speak sharply.
definitions.example: The sergeant barked an order.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to geography (or to geographics)
Determined by geography, as opposed to magnetic (i.e. North)
One who gardens; one who grows plants or cultivates a garden.
definitions.example: "Ponder the fact that God has made you a gardener, to root out vice and to plant virtue." — St. Catherine of Siena

definitions.definition: A cold-blooded vertebrate of the class Reptilia.
A mean or grovelling person.
A gathering of cattle to be branded.
A North American sport involving skills with horses, cows and other livestock.
An entertainment event associated with the sport.
To perform in a rodeo show.
A room or area for preparing food.
Cuisine.
The nape of a person's hairline, often referring to its uncombed or "nappy" look.
The percussion section of an orchestra.
A utensil for roasting meat.
A domesticated or uneducated form of a language.
A public gaming room in a casino.
Anything eaten as a relish with bread, potatoes, etc.
To do kitchen work; to prepare food.
To embellish a basic food; to season, add condiments, etc.
(by extension) To embellish; to dress up.
In a creative manner.
Somebody who is rash, impetuous or impulsive.
A disease spread by mosquito, in which a protozoan, Plasmodium, multiplies in blood every few days.
Supposed poisonous air arising from marshy districts, once thought to cause fever.
To give a nickname to (a person or thing).
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.
definitions.example: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

definitions.definition: (in particular) To wish, desire or demand to see, have the presence of or do business with.
definitions.example: Danish police want him for embezzlement.

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.
definitions.example: You can leave if you want.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).
definitions.example: You’ll want to repeat this three or four times to get the best result.

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).
definitions.example: That chair wants fixing.

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.
definitions.example: There was something wanting in the play.

definitions.definition: To be in a state of destitution; to be needy; to lack.
definitions.example: The paupers desperately want.

definitions.definition: To lack and be without, to not have (something).
definitions.example: She wanted anything she needed.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To lack and (be able to) do without.
That wants or desires.
Absent or lacking.
Deficient.
Without, except, but.
Less, short of, minus.
To make a channel deeper or wider using a dredge.
To bring something to the surface with a dredge.
(Usually with up) to unearth.
definitions.example: to dredge up someone's unsavoury past

definitions.definition: To sprinkle (food) with spices or seasonings, using a dredge.
definitions.example: Dredge the meat with the flour mixture you prepared earlier.

definitions.definition: That which is dredged up.
The act of using a dredger or excavator to dredge a harbour, river, channel or watercourse.
Containing sand or grit; consisting of grit; caused by grit; full of hard particles.
Spirited; resolute; unyielding.
Intense and starkly realistic; depicting harsh reality, especially violence.
Worthy of being remarked or noted; notable
Uncommon; unusual
Rank in taste or smell.
definitions.example: The house was deserted, with a rancid half-eaten meal still on the dinner table.

definitions.definition: Offensive.
definitions.example: His remarks were rancid; everyone got up and left.

definitions.definition: The sense or ability of sight.
Something seen; an object perceived visually.
Something imaginary one thinks one sees.
definitions.example: He tried drinking from the pool of water, but realized it was only a vision.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Something unreal or imaginary; a creation of fancy.
An ideal or a goal toward which one aspires.
definitions.example: He worked tirelessly toward his vision of world peace.

definitions.definition: A religious or mystical experience of a supernatural appearance.
definitions.example: He had a vision of the Virgin Mary.

definitions.definition: A person or thing of extraordinary beauty.
Pre-recorded film or tape; footage.
To imagine something as if it were to be true.
To present as in a vision.
To provide with a vision.
An elongated depression between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it.
The area which drains into a river.
Any structure resembling one, e.g., the meeting point of two pitched roofs.
The internal angle formed by the intersection of two sloping roof planes.
Something which is abrupt; an abyss.
To tear off or asunder.
To interrupt suddenly.
Broken away (from restraint).
Without notice to prepare the mind for the event; sudden; hasty; unceremonious.
definitions.example: The party came to an abrupt end when the parents of our host arrived.

definitions.definition: Curt in manner.
Having sudden transitions from one subject or state to another; unconnected; disjointed.
Broken off.
Extremely steep or craggy as if broken up; precipitous.
Suddenly terminating, as if cut off; truncate.
Any of several animals, in suborder Vermilingua, which are noted for eating ants and termites which they catch with their long sticky tongues.
Any of some other unrelated species that feed with ants, including pangolin (scaly anteater), echidna (spiny anteater), aardvark and numbat (banded anteater).
To make mythical.
The planet Venus as observed in the eastern sky around dawn.
definitions.example: The evening star is the morning star. (Hesperus is Phosphorus.)

definitions.definition: Lucifer, Satan, the devil.
(less commonly) The planet Mercury as observed in the eastern sky around dawn.
A weapon consisting of a heavy ball set with spikes attached rigidly to a staff, in contrast to a flail.
That branch of government which is responsible for maintaining the courts of law and for the administration of justice.
Of or relating to the administration of justice.
Of or relating to the court system or the judicial branch of government.
Specified by a civil bill court under the terms of the Land Law (Ireland) Act, 1881
Of or relating to judgeship or the judiciary, the collective body of judges.
Extending throughout an entire nation.
definitions.example: There was a nationwide search on for the bankrobbers.

definitions.definition: Throughout a nation.
A dome-shaped Inuit shelter, constructed of blocks cut from snow.
A cavity, or excavation, made in the snow by a seal, over its breathing hole in the sea ice.
A reinforced bunker for the storage of nuclear weapons.
(logistics) A kind of airfreight cargo container.
To synchronize, especially in the senses of data synchronization, time synchronization, or synchronizing music with video.
definitions.example: Our cloud storage automatically syncs your files to all your devices.

definitions.definition: To flush all pending I/O operations to disk.
Made holy; set aside for sacred or ceremonial use.
Sanctimonious.
To make holy; to consecrate; to set aside for sacred or ceremonial use.
To free from sin; to purify.
To make acceptable or useful under religious law or practice.
To endorse with religious sanction.
A cannon that combines certain characteristics of guns and mortars. The howitzer delivers projectiles with medium velocities, by either low or high trajectories.

Normally a cannon with a tube length of 20 to 30 calibers; however, the tube length can exceed 30 calibers and still be considered a howitzer when the high angle fire zoning solution permits range overlap between charges
A powerfully hit shot.
